slides.01: You spent months making your film. On release day, someone records it in the theatre. Within hours it spreads on WhatsApp, Telegram, YouTube. In just one week — crores in revenue are gone. And you can do nothing about it.
slides.02: Piracy is killing the film industry. Producers are losing 60 to 80 percent of their revenue. There is no way to control it. Removing links manually takes days. There is no court evidence. Distributors lose trust. This is the reality every producer faces.
slides.03: What if piracy stopped automatically — without you doing anything? That system exists. It detects piracy, proves it, and removes it — all on its own. It is called the Movie Anti-Piracy Protection System.
slides.04: This system has four layers. IR Detection makes phone cameras useless in the theatre. TPVM destroys any recording completely. Watermarking identifies exactly which theatre a leak came from. And the Takedown System removes pirated content from the internet automatically, 24 by 7.
slides.05: 940 nanometer infrared LEDs are installed around the screen — invisible to the human eye. The audience sees the film perfectly. But any phone camera picks up the IR light and the recording turns into a completely white, unusable flash. No matter how expensive the phone — the footage is destroyed.
slides.06: TPVM stands for Temporal Pixel Visual Masking. Seven invisible layers are injected into every frame during projection. The audience sees nothing different. But a phone camera captures each layer separately — producing a flickering, scrambled, completely unwatchable video. No recording device can beat it.
slides.07: Every screen, every show gets a unique invisible watermark in every frame. The audience cannot see it. But if a pirated copy is found online — the watermark reveals exactly which theatre, which screen, and which show it came from. It is court-admissible evidence.
slides.08: An automated scanner monitors YouTube, Telegram, Facebook, Instagram, and torrent sites continuously. The moment a pirated video appears, AI detects it within minutes. A DMCA notice is filed automatically. Content is removed — no human needed. Thousands of links handled every single day.
slides.09: To enable automatic takedowns, API access is needed from platforms. Four documents are required — a Digital Rights Certificate proving ownership, Content ID to fingerprint your film on YouTube, Metadata with film details and ISAN number, and Documentation like distribution agreements. Submit these once — and all future takedowns happen instantly.
slides.10: You get a real-time dashboard showing exactly how many piracy links were detected, removed, and pending — broken down by platform. Instant alerts the moment a new link is found. Everything runs automatically. You do nothing — your film is protected around the clock.
slides.11: Before — 80 percent revenue lost, no control, days of manual work, no court evidence. After — revenue protected from day one, automatic piracy removal 24 by 7, court-ready evidence always available, and you do nothing at all. Your film is safe from the moment it releases.
